llm_calls.content_preview:
Phase 2 complete — CHANNELS created. Here’s the CHANNEL_MAP and next-step plan. I’m flagging an inconsistency for your approval before we seed content.

CHANNEL_MAP
- Ch1: Apple Platform & Product Upd

arguments.name: Apple Corporate Intranet
arguments.description: Apple’s internal hub crafted to reflect the company’s design ethos: simple, premium, and human-centered. The space aligns product leadership with privacy, security, and global operations—a single source of truth for executives, engineers, retail teams, and corporate functions.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to Apple’s internal hub—an intentionally simple, human-centered space where teams collaborate, protect privacy, and move fast to ship great products. Design is how we work, not just how we look.

arguments.name: Apple Platform & Product Updates
arguments.description:
Public-facing updates on platform changes, OS integrations, chipsets, and developer ecosystems that power Apple’s products.

This channel keeps internal teams aligned on product timing, compatibility, and strategic direction.

frequent_questions.question: How will platform updates affect my team’s roadmap?
frequent_questions.answer: Updates outline expected timelines, compatibility notes, and integration points so teams can adjust plans with confidence.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find developer tooling or SDK changes?
frequent_questions.answer: Refer to the internal developer portal linked in the channel header and the weekly engineering sync notes.

frequent_questions.question: How are platform changes communicated to non-engineering teams?
frequent_questions.answer: Cross-functional briefs and leadership memos are distributed through the intranet and the weekly all-hands summary.

frequent_questions.question: Who approves major platform changes?
frequent_questions.answer: The platform leadership council, with input from product, security, and program management.

frequent_questions.question: What is the expected impact on timelines?
frequent_questions.answer: Timelines are aligned with the product roadmap; impact details are captured in each update post.

arguments.name: Apple Intelligence & Vision Pro
arguments.description: Updates on AI features, Vision Pro ecosystem, hardware-software integration, and developer tools. Focus on responsible AI and on-device processing.

frequent_questions.question: What new capabilities are coming this quarter?
frequent_questions.answer: High-level milestones and API changes are summarized in each post.

frequent_questions.question: How does on-device processing protect privacy?
frequent_questions.answer: Explanation of processing paths and data minimization practices.

frequent_questions.question: Where can developers access new tools for Vision Pro?
frequent_questions.answer: Developer portal and SDK release notes are linked in the post.

frequent_questions.question: What are the privacy implications of AI features across platforms?
frequent_questions.answer: On-device processing with strict privacy safeguards; data handling follows company policy.

arguments.name: Leadership & Strategy Letters
arguments.description: Executive updates, strategic context, and governance notes from the CEO and executive leadership. A trusted space for leadership communications.

frequent_questions.question: Who should read these letters?
frequent_questions.answer: All executives and managers, plus anyone needing strategic context.

frequent_questions.question: How often will leadership messages be published?
frequent_questions.answer: Regular cadence aligned with quarterly business reviews and milestones.

frequent_questions.question: Are these messages confidential?
frequent_questions.answer: Some content is restricted to leadership teams; others are public-facing for staff-wide alignment.

frequent_questions.question: How can I provide feedback on strategy updates?
frequent_questions.answer: Feedback channels through leadership office and town halls.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find the annual strategic priorities?
frequent_questions.answer: In quarterly reviews and the leadership letters archive.

arguments.name: ESG, Privacy & Compliance
arguments.description: Policy, governance, and privacy updates; compliance requirements, supplier responsibility, and regulatory context. Requires acknowledgment where noted.

frequent_questions.question: What must I acknowledge?
frequent_questions.answer: Key policies and changes require read confirmation; see the article for specifics.

frequent_questions.question: How do I report potential non-compliance?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the compliance channel’s escalation path and supervisor guidance.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find the full privacy program?
frequent_questions.answer: Privacy program overview is published in the channel and linked to the Privacy page.

frequent_questions.question: What is the process for supplier compliance?
frequent_questions.answer: Supplier standards and audit results are published here and in the Supplier Portal.

frequent_questions.question: How often are privacy and security trainings?
frequent_questions.answer: Training cadence is published in the quarterly security briefings.

arguments.name: Retail & Global Operations
arguments.description:
Global store operations, logistics, and retail experience with an emphasis on consistency and regional adaptation.

This channel shares regional updates, store standards, and operational excellence practices to maintain a unified brand experience.

frequent_questions.question: How are stores aligned globally?
frequent_questions.answer: Brand standards, training material, and regional updates are synchronized via this channel.

frequent_questions.question: How do we escalate regional issues?
frequent_questions.answer: Regional ops leads and the central ops team coordinate on escalation posts.

frequent_questions.question: What standards govern the in-store experience?
frequent_questions.answer: Brand guidelines, merchandising policies, and customer-service training are published here.

frequent_questions.question: How can stores request support from central Ops?
frequent_questions.answer: Submit requests through the central Ops channel and regional coordinators will respond.

frequent_questions.question: Are there regional performance dashboards I should monitor?
frequent_questions.answer: Regional dashboards and KPIs are linked in posts and the channel index.

arguments.name: People & Culture
arguments.description: Employee resources, ERGs, inclusion initiatives, and culture programs. Highlighting people-first stories and internal recognition.

frequent_questions.question: What ERGs exist and how can I join?
frequent_questions.answer: ERG list provided in this channel; joining guidance is posted in each group’s page.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find health and well-being resources?
frequent_questions.answer: Wellness programs are linked in the People & Culture hub and on the main benefits page.

frequent_questions.question: How are employee recognitions shared?
frequent_questions.answer: Spotlight articles and monthly town halls highlight achievements.

frequent_questions.question: What opportunities exist for career development within Apple?
frequent_questions.answer: Training programs, mentorship, and rotation opportunities are posted here.

frequent_questions.question: How can I nominate a colleague for recognition?
frequent_questions.answer: Submit nominations through the recognition form linked in the channel.

frequent_questions.question: How can I nominate a colleague for recognition?
